name: Evidence Ledger Memo
description: A calm static memo page that shows how every claim maps to a verifiable artifact.
brief: Create an editorial ledger style static site for a credibility-focused service memo. It should feel like a polished briefing, not a dashboard. Pair claims with evidence, artifacts, contracts, QA output, and deployment receipts. Keep it static with no analytics, no payments, no fake quoted endorsements, no fake metrics, and no live data.
heroHeadline: A calm memo where every claim has provenance
heroLede: This editorial ledger turns service positioning into a readable static memo: claims, evidence, sections, and boundaries stay close enough for future agents to audit.
audience: Founders who want credible service positioning
Reviewers checking whether claims are supported
Agents maintaining static copy without inventing proof
visualDirection: light editorial memo with paper texture, serif headlines, numbered entries, and claim ledger rows

sections.title: Claim provenance before persuasion
sections.body: The page treats every strong line as something that needs a source, not just a better adjective.

sections.title: Future maintainers need a short audit trail
sections.body: Contracts, QA scripts, screenshots, runbooks, and deploy receipts make it easier for agents to improve copy without drifting.

sections.title: The limits are part of the trust surface
sections.body: The memo says what is not included: no live data, no payment flow, no analytics, no fabricated customer proof, and no unsupported guarantees.

proofArtifacts.label: Site contract
proofArtifacts.body: .agent/site.contract.yaml records allowed claims, required sections, and approval boundaries.

proofArtifacts.label: QA scripts
proofArtifacts.body: npm run qa checks contracts, claims, links, SEO basics, and the production build.

proofArtifacts.label: Visual QA screenshots
proofArtifacts.body: npm run test:visual verifies desktop and mobile layouts and saves inspection screenshots.

proofArtifacts.label: Deploy verification
proofArtifacts.body: npm run verify:deploy confirms the GitHub Pages URL contains expected live copy.
